name: Acrobat's Edge
description: Trivia Despite being called a katana in the description, this item appears to be a Naginata (“なぎなた” or “薙刀“), a type of polearm originally used by the samurai class of feudal Japan.

name: Acropolis Armor
description: Notes Long ago, this armor used to only drop from Oryx the Mad God 2, and with few good alternatives for a melee class to wear, this armor had a very high trade value. Now, however, there are several viable untiered alternatives, as well as the higher tiered Dominion Armor and Annihilation Armor completely outclassing it. This has led to a significant reduction in its trade value over time, as with many other “wine cellar tops.”

name: Adamantine Helm
description: History Before Exalt Version 6.8.0.0 (Mar 2026), this item was soulbound.

name: Advent Seal
description: Notes Although not an ST item in its own right, this item is classed as Oryxmas Gear, and, if worn in conjuntion with Ornaments of Unity, will contribute to the Oryxmas Gear bonus stats.
effects: On party: Healing, Damaging and HP Boost HP Boost: +70 (+2 per WIS over 50) max HP HP Boost Range: 4.5 (+1 per 10 WIS over 50) tiles HP Boost Duration: 4 (+1 per 10 WIS over 50) seconds Healing & Damaging Range: 4.5 (+0.1 per WIS over 50) tiles Healing & Damaging Duration: 4 (+0.1 per WIS over 50) seconds

name: Adventurer's Scarf
description: Notes This UT ring is an incredibly rare biome white, available from any rookie biome in The Realm.
effects: Trusty Companion: On ability use, heal 75 HP Cooldown: 15 seconds

name: Aegis Armor
description: Notes Few other heavy armors give much of an attack boost, so 4 ATT is already decent. Compared to the Fire Dragon Battle Armor, Aegis Armor gives +4 DEF at the cost of -2 ATT, FDA’s other stats, and the fire proc. However, Mad God’s Rage grants a great increase to DPS, especially where you’d be taking lots of damage anyway (e.g. exaltation dungeons, such as Lost Halls and Fungal Cavern). The effect alone adds the same DPS stats that the Gladiator Guard has, which is very powerful indeed.
effects: Mad God’s Rage: Gain ATT Boost (+5 ATT) and DEX Boost (+5 DEX) for 3 seconds when hit under 80% HP.

name: An Icicle
description: Notes This is a reskinned version of the T11 Agateclaw Dagger. During the Oryxmas season, this weapon will drop wherever the Agateclaw Dagger would normally drop.

name: Anatis Staff
description: Notes The Giant Chicken is typically not in the game, only on April Fool’s Day, Oryx the Mad God is replaced by the Giant Oryx Chicken. During the rest of the year, it cannot be found, making its drops rare.

name: Ancient Eminence
description: Notes Part of the Twilight Archmage Set.
effects: With Defiled Equilibrium: Summons Inferno for 4.8 seconds that deals 130-150 armor piercing damage Summons Blizzard for 4.8 seconds that deals 90-100 damage and inflicts Slowed for 3 seconds Cooldown: 5.8 seconds Ignores defense of target

name: Ancient Stone Sword
description: Notes The Ancient Stone Sword is useful for when it will one-shot monsters, or against extreme defense. Otherwise, despite the high base damage, it performs poorly. It loses to the T11 Skysplitter Sword until well past 100 DEF. With the introduction of the armor piercing Oryx’s Greatsword, the A.S.S no longer is the best for DPS in any situation.

name: Angel's Fanfare
description: Notes Part of the Angelic Bard Set.
effects: Party Effect: Within 4 squares Inspired for 4 seconds.Party Effect: Within 4 squares HP Boost (+80 HP) for 4 seconds.If Harmonious Harp equipped: ATT Boost (+8 ATT) on self for 4 seconds (cooldown: 0.5 seconds)If Wings of Sanctity equipped: DEF Boost (+12 DEF) on self for 4 seconds (cooldown: 0.5 seconds)If Heavenly Halo equipped: Within 4 squares Healing for 4 seconds (cooldown: 0.5 seconds)

name: Annihilation Armor
description: Notes A clear-cut upgrade over the Candy Coated Armor, this end-game armor offers a massive increase in defense without compromise.

name: Arbiter's Wrath
description: Notes The Arbiter’s Wrath drops in the Battle for the Nexus dungeon, which was released for a limited time in September 2013 and occasionally reappears during events.

name: Arcane Rapier
description: Notes Although the DPS of this sword is nearly on par with (slightly worse than) the Crystal Sword’s, this sword pierces, has twice the projectile velocity, and boomerangs back to its origin once reaching max range. It is therefore good for clearing out groups of enemies as well as general accuracy. Note that you can only hit each individual enemy once per shot; you can’t use the boomerang to hit twice.
effects: Shots hit multiple targets Shots boomerang

name: Armor of Nil
description: Notes This armor currently grants the most DEF in the game, higher than the T14 heavy armor, and equal to the Candy-Coated Armor. This is in exchange for a small mobility penalty and lack of DPS boost. Compared to the Leviathan Armor, this armor is essentially a -7 DEX & -5 SPD penalty for +9 DEF boost.
effects: Planar Absorption: When hit reduce incoming damage taken by 15% for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds

name: Ase's Echo
description: Notes The mark stays even after teleporting, but is removed after using the ability after the second activation.
effects: First activation: Mark the location creating Berserk aura in a radius of3. Second activation: Teleport back to the marked location exploding the previous mark dealing 1000 damage and granting Armored for 5 seconds in a radius of 5. Note: Mark is reset upon changing area or after 10 seconds.

name: Ashen Talon
description: Notes The Ashen Talon is the only dagger in the game that both completely ignores the defense of the target and pierces through enemies. As a result, this dagger is useful in Tombs and The Shatters where high defense enemies are found. It is also effective at realm events such as the Cube God or Skull Shrine where it can be used to shred through large swarms of satellite minions.

name: Astronomer's Gown
description: Notes Part of the Astral Arcanist Set.
effects: On Ability Use: Gain Energized for 3.6 seconds. Empowerment: Duration is 5.6 seconds while wearing Eye of Divination.

name: Atavistic Soul Sabre
description: Notes The Atavistic Soul Sabre is an upgrade of the Ancient Stone Sword with higher shot damage. It has more DPS than the T13 Sword of Splendor at all levels of enemy defense. It outdamages the T14 Sword of Majesty at > 50 DEF and the Sword of the Colossus at > 100 DEF.

name: Augur of the Tempest
description: Notes Part of the Storm Caller Bard Set.
effects: Concussive Waves: Grants additional recursive thunder explosions to the lightning strike of Thundering Chorus

name: Autarch Amulet
description: Notes Wearing this ring with other items of the Agents of Oryx series will grant additional set bonuses depending on how many pieces are worn. Out of the three rings, this one is specialized for utility, granting decent defensive bonuses while providing large amounts of MP and WIS for ability casting when powered up.

name: Avarice
description: Notes A dagger with great DPS, above the T14 Dagger of Dire Hatred, and high range, 0.38 tiles greater than tiered. The tradeoff is that its shots alternate left and right, much like the shots of the Sword of the Colossus (though to a lesser extent). This makes it hard to hit enemies in the middle of your range. If you can master this quirk, Avarice will be one of the strongest daggers in the game, safe to use as your main dagger.

While its ability to Stun is devastating (as of its release, this makes Ninja the only class besides the Knight that can Stun foes), the Inner Circle’s pitiful range of 1.5 tiles mean that the Ninja has to get dangerous

This allows the Void bow to deal more consistent damage from a safer distance.

name: Brain Cube
description: Notes Unlike tiered skulls, the Brain Cube does not have a Vampire Blast ability. In exchange, the minion it summons rapidly deals 4 hits of heavy defense-ignoring damage in an expanding area of effect with a generous 10% lifesteal. As the lifesteal is applied to each instance of damage, the Brain Cube can rapidly heal players if multiple enemies are caught in the blast radius.

Since you need to hit enemies at the maximum range for high DPS, positioning yourself to maximize the shots hitting the target is recommended.

description: Notes A bow that is overwhelming against stationary enemies or those with predictable movement, but rather pitiful against those with erratic movement or lategame bosses that often force you away from the 5 tile sweet spot. It can be used as a main bow for clearing enemies, despite feeling somewhat worse than tiered bows at times due to the requirement to always play around 5 tiles. The damage is mediocre when rushing dungeons. Have a swapout for when you are unable to stay at 5 tiles range.
effects: Shots hit multiple targets Shots pass through obstacles
Shots hit multiple targets Shots pass through obstacles Ignores defense of target

name: Centaur's Shielding
description: Notes This armor is similar to the Hirejou Tenne in that it sacrifices much of its DEF for a hefty increase in damage. The base damage stats are less than those of the Tenne, which provides +8 ATT/DEX. However, the Centaur’s Shielding is more potent than the Tenne when the user is inflicted with Healing, most commonly in group situations where Paladins or Priests are common.
effects: On Shoot: ATT Boost (+12 ATT) on self for 4.5 seconds when the user has the Healing status effect Cooldown: 6 seconds

name: Centipede Poison

Forbidden Secrets: 40% Chance on ability to Curse the user for 4 seconds.Cooldown: 5 seconds.

name: Chain Dagger
description: Notes Although this dagger has a relatively poor DPS for its level, it inflicts significant Bleed buildup. It also builds up dexterity as it fires, which helps offset the naturally low RoF. Bleed comprises most of this weapon’s power, so it is advised to use other DEX-boosting gear to maximize it.

name: Chancellor's Cranium
description: Notes The Chancellor’s Cranium is a well balanced offensive and defensive ring. It provides valuable defensive stats such as +120 HP and +6 DEF, while also providing a small stat bonus of +3 DEX and a proc which has the opportunity to do a large amount of DPS (+750 optimally). Although there are better defensive and offensive rings, the Cranium can serve as a highly effective balance between the two.
effects: On Taking Damage: Shots above 50 damage summon a following cranium which deals 150 damage every 0.2 seconds for 5 seconds and zaps enemies in a radius of 6 squares Cooldown: 5 seconds

name: Chaotic Scripture
description: Notes This tome’s ability to instantly strike anywhere within the user’s sight range with a devastating, low cooldown, armor-piercing AoE attack, makes it unrivaled among tomes in terms of increasing DPS. This ability can single-handedly turn a Priest into a long-distance damage machine, good against groups and bosses alike.
effects: Heal: 90 (+0.715 per ATT over 60) HP Damage Nova: Explodes once within 2.5 sqrs

name: Cheerful Chipper
description: Notes A unique sword that fires spreads of 3 projectiles that rapidly decelerate until stationary. This sword out damages both the Sword of the Colossus and Pixie-Enchanted Sword from 5 to 40 Defense respectively, making it a very strong competitor to most end-game swords. This, however, is offset by its short range (and even shorter true range) and slightly lowered fire rate, in addition to its peculiar shot pattern making it harder to use.

name: Chicken Leg of Doom
description: Notes The Giant Oryx Chicken is typically only available during the April Fool’s Day event, where it replaces Oryx the Mad God. Outside of April Fool’s Day, the Chicken does not spawn, making its novelty drops rare.

name: Cloak of the Mad God
description: Notes An extremely unconventional cloak that sacrifices a large portion of its invisibility time for the ability to Armor its user for 5 seconds upon use. The +50% DEF the buff gives its user can be a godsend in situations where shots are being fired regardless of your invisibility, or if you uncloak in an unfortunate spot (since it lasts longer than your invisibility). After the Cloak rework was introduced, this remains the only Cloak that allows you to shoot without ending Invisibility, making it a great cloak to pair with the Collector’s Monocle.
effects: Shooting with lethal strike: Grants Armored buff for 5 seconds After exiting sneak: Fire 2 additional projectiles dealing damage equal to your Lethal Strike damage for 2.4 seconds. description: Notes Since this cloak has the same cooldown as the Snake Eye Ring, it provides the highest speed stat bonus possible of the cloaks that allow the most effecient application of the speedy proc. However, based on overall effective speed, it still falls short to the Planewalker, since teleportation is much faster than speedy.

name: Cloak of the Planewalker
description: Because of the ability to teleport, lack of invisibility cooldown, and high speed bonus, this cloak is the best for rushing dungeons. It is also commonly used to avoid dangerous situations that would normally lead to players either dying or nexusing. This makes the cloak a must-have swap-out for every Rogue.

name: Cloak of the Red Agent
description: Notes This cloak is sometimes used as a replacement from the higher tiered cloaks due to the fact that its lower MP cost can let a player using it cloak more times in a row (and thus for a longer duration) than if they were to use a T5 or T6.

name: Clockwork Repeater
description: Notes A bow with high damage and high true range. This is compensated by the slow rate of fire, which makes it harder to aim. It beats the Bow of the Void starting at ~24 DEF, Warmonger at 27/24/22 DEF, and the T14 Bow of Deep Enchantment at 34/31/28 DEF. Note that the T14 bow will outdamage at any reasonable defense if the target is Exposed, although it requires the user to be much closer to hit all shots.

name: Clover Star
description: Notes A vanity star, which can replace the T0 Basic Star for players that use Speedy in short bursts often, to prevent additional loss of MP.

name: Cnidaria Rod
description: Notes This scepter is very unique for its increase in damage with each additional target, while other scepters have lower damage each time you hit an enemy. This and its low base damage - which is half of Scepter of Storm’s and the same as Scepter of Fulmination’s - make it best used as a swapout when large crowds of enemies are present.

name: Cocoon Quiver
description: Notes This, along with the Bramble Bow, is a great early to mid game archer item. It drops from an early dungeon, and paralyzes! The paralyze is not as long as the higher tiered quivers (3 seconds vs 2 seconds), but having 2 shots makes landing your paralyze easier. The DPS is also comparable to a T6 quiver if both shots hit, while costing 15 less mana per use.

name: Collector's Monocle
description: Notes The Monocle gives sublime HP and DEX boosts (equivalent to the T5 rings of each stat), but it’s main appeal is its massive +20 DEX total if you can go invisible. Assuming you can, the proc has an effective 100% uptime, with a long duration compared to its cooldown or the Cloak cooldown. A consistent Monocle far surpasses otherwise endgame rings like The Forgotten Crown.

name: Combcutter Kunai
description: Notes Compared to tiered versions, this star gives up some of the Ninja’s trademark Speed for higher DPS, subtracting 10 Speed on equip and trading Speedy for Damaging. Furthermore, it provides an ATT Boost while held and fires additional kunai when in combat and when below 75% HP. The additional kunai fire at an awkward angle when using Tachis, about 22.5 degrees to the left of where the player is aiming.

description: Notes This is a very powerful orb that can inflict one of the longest curses in the game. This is the best orb for converting mana into raw damage, especially with the 5.13 update removing the quiet effect.

name: Conducting Wand
description: Notes The Conducting Wand’s +4 DEX and high rate of fire gives it enough DPS to even slightly outdamage the T14 Wand of Retribution until 49/41/33 DEF, and beats the T12 Wand of Recompense at any reasonable DEF. The extra wisdom also boosts the Priest’s and Sorcerer’s abilities. This power comes at the cost of range (1 tile shorter) and piercing; it is recommended to keep a tiered wand for when those traits are helpful.

name: Consecrated Trap
description: Notes Part of the Cursed Cursebreaker Huntress Set.

name: Coral Bow
description: Notes The Coral Bow is comparable to the T12 Bow of Covert Havens. The Coral Bow has a longer true range and higher projectile speed than tiered bows (6.35 tiles compared to 4.07 for tiered bows), but is outdamaged by the T12 when all shots hit, outside of very low DEF. When two shots hit from the T12, it will outdamage the Coral Bow at 54/48/43 DEF.

name: Coral Ring
description: Notes This ring is very rarely used by players unless they are trying to complete the Coral Huntress Set. This is because it does not give any defensive stats.
effects: Coral Blessing: Reduces the Magic casting cost of your Ability by 10%

name: Coral Silk Armor
description: Notes While not as defensively focused as tiered leather armors, this armor is balanced out by having more dexterity, as well as an equally sizeable Wisdom boost. If you want a more utility-focused build but don’t want to fully commit to an armor such as the Harlequin Armor, you might find this armor to be a good middle ground.

name: Coral Soda

name: Coral Venom Trap
description: Notes The Coral Venom Trap inflicts Paralyzed. Against vulnerable enemies, paralysis is a much more potent status than the usual Slowed. Traps also have the benefit of being AOE, as opposed to the Archer’s Quivers. However, many lategame bosses are immune to paralysis, so you should bring another trap as a swapout.

name: Corsair Ring
description: Notes Being one of the first UT rings a player may obtain, this ring has twice as much DEF as T0 Ring of Minor Defense while having two-thirds the health of a Ring of Health, making it useful for maintaining a healthy amount of HP and DEF early game.

name: Corsair's Crown Jewel
description: Notes This unique prism summons following prisms that do not decoy until they activate, making it a bad choice for decoying enemies that approach quickly. Instead, this prism proves itself very useful while dragging enemies that chase the player, as the prisms follow the player’s path until they activate.

It gives 5 HP more compared to Essence Tap Skull, but the summon does a bit less damage, and lacks Vampiric Blast. So it’s not advisable to use over any other skull (other than Heartstealer Skull or less tiered ones).

name: Crest of the Rising Sun
description: Notes At first glance, this bow is incredible. It outdamages every bow at low to mid DEF, being only beaten by a 5-shot Predator Bow. It has one of the highest ranges of bows at 8 tiles. Unlike tiered bows, the arc gap is small enough to consistently make use of the full normal range. While the Crest of the Rising Sun’s true range isn’t boosted by the Bard’s Inspired, the range still surpasses its endgame competitor, the Bow of the Void. It is also great for crowd control, piercing enemies and passing through obstacles.

description: Please note this content is designed solely for the use of admins and cannot be obtained by normal players.

name: Crystal Bone Ring
description: Notes This ring is very rarely used as a primary ring, because of its lack of defensive stats. Tier 2 Rings are just as easy to find, and give better stats.
effects: On Shoot: 10% chance to restore 5 MP Cooldown: 0.4 seconds

name: Crystal Key
description: Notes While this ring’s stats are relatively mediocre compared to other rings of its rarity, it has a chance to release a ring of key bullets when the user is hit. The key nova inflicts good damage, as well as Slowing and Exposing all enemies it hits, making it decent for crippling nearby foes. However, due to the unreliability of the proc, combined with the ring’s stats, this is a ring that is somewhat outclassed in many situations, although it may prove useful to cripple enemies while rushing.
effects: Keyper’s Trickery: Releases a barrage of 10 keys to hinder opponents On Taking Damage: 10% chance to create key nova Key Damage: 100 Key Bullet Speed: 8.5 tiles/second Key Range: 4.25 tiles Key Effect: Slowed for 2s, Exposed for 4s Cooldown: 5 seconds

name: Crystal Mace
description: Notes Along with the Stonemould Mace, this is currently only one of two maces to ignore the defense of its target, making it a solid choice for cracking high-DEF enemies that would normally shrug off other summons. While its total base damage is higher than many other maces, the steeds’ reliance on hitting all their (individually weaker) shots crimps its DPS somewhat, especially against enemies with low DEF that would generally take more damage from other maces.
effects: Shots pass through obstacles Ignores defense of target

name: Crystal Shield
description: Notes A bizarre and completely unique shield that functions similarly to a Ninja star.
effects: While Armored: On taking damage, fire a shot in retaliation. While Armored: On taking 30 or more damage, heal 10 HP.

name: Crystal Sword
description: Notes The Crystal Sword is a utility weapon with the highest practical range of non-vanity swords, one tile higher than tiered options. The Pixie-Enchanted Sword and Divinity’s beams have higher range but do not hit all shots at that range. It exchanges damage in the process, being even weaker than the T8 Ravenheart Sword. Despite being noticably weaker, CSword allows players to more easily hit dangerous-to-approach enemies like Oryx the Mad God 2.

name: Crystal Wand
description: Notes This wand’s damage ignores defense, making it good for fighting enemies/bosses with high amounts of defense, or those protected with the Armored status effect. Examples include the Avatar of the Forgotten King, the Puppet Master, the Stone Guardians, and the Tormented Golem. One of the best places to use this wand is in the Parasite Chambers, as the boss of this dungeon has 5,000 defense until it is armor broken.
effects: Shots hit multiple targets Ignores defense of target

name: Crystalline Kunai
description: Notes Part of the Crystal Kunoichi Set.
effects: Heightened Senses: Retaliate with 3 kunais when hit. Kunai Cooldown: 4 seconds.

name: Crystallised Fang's Venom
description: Notes This poison sacrifices personal damage for the unique and very powerful effect of inflicting Armor Broken, which can massively increase group damage. Unlike the Knight’s Shield of Ogmur, Assassins can Armor Break from a distance. The downside is the very long throw time and small radius, which can make it difficult to aim at mobile targets.
effects: On enemies: Armor Broken for 4 seconds

name: Crystallised Mist
description: Notes This ring should be used to further enhance the rushing ability of a class such as Warrior or Trickster, as it allows the user to become invisible for 8 seconds upon taking a bullet that deals 50 or more damage. It also provides reasonably high bonuses to SPD and DEF to further aid rushing prowess, with the HP being a nice bonus.
effects: On Taking Damage: Invisible for 8 seconds Damage Threshold: Damage > 50 Cooldown: 40 seconds

name: Crystallized Worm Spellblade
description: Notes This staff fires 4 shots total; two shots on each side. Each side, like other spellblades, has a piercing/armor piercing shot and a more damaging shot. Shots start spreading out at the start of its range, and then converge as they travel further. They do not fully converge without Inspired, but it’s still always possible to hit all shots from the end of its natural range. Notably, it nearly matches the shot pattern of the Primal Arcana.

name: Deliverance
description: Notes While seeming strong at first glance, the curse and damage dealing effect from the forgotten souls can be unreliable unless at close distance. To take full advantage of the damage dealing potential this wand has, especially for fighting bosses, one should try to position themselves to drag the souls within the path enemy movement. Using this wand while attempting to stay near bosses and enemies with chase phases in particular can be quite strong compared with other late-game wand options that may rely more on player aim.

name: Demon Blade
description: Notes The Demon Blade stands out among the UT Swords for its signature two-shot, V-shaped firing pattern creating a 25 degree angle. It has a short true range, but if you can hit both shots, it’s one of the strongest weapons in the game. It beats all swords except the Pixie-Enchanted Sword, and Pixie is only stronger against 0 DEF targets.

name: Dirk of Cronus
description: As funny as it has been to watch the increasing fervor and rampant speculation about dirk rarity in these forums, I’m becoming worried that some poor soul might actually get tricked into trading away things of great value in exchange for a dirk, so let me officially put a stop to the silliness: no, dirks are not becoming ultra-rare or ultra-valuable. The root of this rumor seems to be this thread, where many of players were joking around. Rest assured, it truly was just a joke.

name: Divination Cloak
description: Notes A very medicore leather armor when looked at by itself, compared to the T12 Hydra Skin Armor it sacrifices 2 defence for an added 50 MP. This armor only shines when used with the Invocation Scroll, as the Divine Rage passive will be used, and the ST set bonus will help offset the lost def when compared to the Hydra Armor.

name: Divine Coronation
description: Notes A direct upgrade to the Ring of the Pyramid, the Divine Coronation is a powerful ring with a nice array of offense and defense. Compared to the similar status Bracer of the Guardian, the Coro gives +20 HP and better DPS in exchange for less DEF and MP. It’s a fairly strong ring for any class, an alternative to other top-tier rings such as the Forgotten Crown, Omnipotence Ring, or Exalted God’s Horn.

description: Notes Boasts the third-highest defense of all the heavy armors. Since one can obtain this armor through multiple end-game dungeons, it remains a powerful defensive option.

name: Doom Bow
description: Notes The Doom Bow has a very high damage / shot, making it able to one-shot many enemies. It is great against high DEF enemies due to its low firerate and high damage. The main drawback of this bow comes from said firerate. It makes accuracy more important, as each missed shot makes a bigger difference. It also makes it harder to hit fast moving enemies.

And thus this item will most likely stay a vanity item for a while.

description: Notes A direct upgrade to the Experimental Ring, this ring inherits and enhances its worth as a potent all-rounded ring for boosting survivability. However, the E.Y.E. adds another strength on top of that by firing a projectile counterattack whenever the user takes a hit. This counterattack fires a decelerating shot in the direction of the enemy that dealt the hit that triggered it.

name: Earthen Ward
description: Notes This powerful wand has a peculiar shot pattern that loosely resembles a peace symbol (☮). The shots go outwards and create a “ring”, because of the ring shaped pattern, it is technically possible to hit at least two shots at full range, however it is only possible to hit all shots if you are sitting on top of enemies, similar to the Staff of Extreme Prejudice. If you manage to hit all shots, this weapon completely outdamages Lumiaire.

Instead, this prism proves itself very useful while dragging enemies that chase the player, as the prisms follow the player’s path until they activate.

name: Gambler's Fate
description: Notes A unique “prism” that doesn’t teleport the player, but rather puts a stationary coin decoy on the cursor’s location. While the length of the decoy is highly inconsistent due to using a luck-based system to determine its duration (≈ 4s*), the ability to plant the decoy exactly wherever the user wishes from a safe distance (unlike the Brain of the Golem, which also deploys a stationary decoy, except on the user’s current position) allows Tricksters to be far more precise with their decoy placements than with any other prism. This makes it a highly useful tool for setting up precise decoys,
effects: On Ability Use: Summons a coin that acts as a decoy. Each second it has a 50% chance to become silver or continue to exist. Silver Coin: Acts as a decoy. Each second it has a 50% chance to disappear or continue to exist.

It does have a small negative reactive proc in the form of Hallucinating for 1.5 seconds upon hit at a 5% chance, but this effect has a 20 second cooldown, so the Hallucinating effect is negligible.

effects: Party Effect: Within 4.5 squares Inspired for 4 seconds. Jester Cannon: Throws a mischievous jester at your target. Jester Throw Time: 0.6s Mischievous Jester: 450 damage within 2 squares every 1 second. (1800 maximum total) Mischievous Jester Effect: Ignores defense of target
Jester Frenzy: 5% chance upon shooting for a mischievous jester to protect you for 3 seconds.

name: Judge's Robe
description: Notes Compared to T14 Robe of the Star Mother, the Judge’s Robe suffers a deficit of -5 MP, -4 DEF, and -2 ATT in exchange for a mere +1 WIS advantage. But as Mad God’s Rage was increased from 300 HP to 80% of maximum HP, players can get an extra offense boost much more easily. This tends to work best in the likes of most exaltation dungeons (e.g. Lost Halls, The Nest, Fungal Cavern), as they deal enough damage to activate Mad God’s Rage without necessarily putting the user at risk of death.
effects: Mad God’s Rage: Gain ATT Boost (+5 ATT) and DEX Boost (+5 DEX) for 3 seconds when hit under 80% HP.

description: Notes This star sacrifices its Speedy ability and quite a bit of range for piercing and potentially massive damage that’s stronger the closer you are to targets. Additionally, the extra +5 defense can be beneficial.

description: Notes A powerful utility ring which grants a balance of survivability and spellcasting ability, alongside summoning spirits that can aid in damage output, crowd control, and healing, albeit with some RNG involved.
effects: Reduces the Magic casting cost of your ability by 10%

On Ability: Summon a level 2 assorted Friendly Spirit. The spirit synergizes in the presence of different spirits. Pink Spirit: Heals allies and attacks enemies within 3 squares. Blue Spirit: Slows and attacks enemies within 3 squares. Yellow Spirit: Curses and attacks enemies within 3 squares. Lifetime: 6 seconds Cooldown: 1.5 seconds

name: Kendo Stick
description: Notes This item’s shiny variant is one of, if not the rarest items to obtain, and unlike other Shiny Items, this variant has a guaranteed chance to drop with Divine rarity, similarly to Jewel Eye Katana (Shiny).

name: Keychain Cutlass
description: Notes The Keychain Cutlass has the same average damage as the Sword of Acclaim, but has a massive damage variation. This makes killing low HP minions unreliable, but marginally helps against high DEF enemies. Against bosses, it’s generally an upgrade from the Acclaim thanks to the bonus 50 MP, 5 VIT, and 3 DEF, as well as its +3% XP bonus.

name: Kiritsukeru
description: Notes A katana that sacrifices fire rate and shot speed for a great increase in damage and a slight range boost. It is best used to instantly kill minions with the higher damage per shot, such as various Demons in the Abyss of Demons.

name: KoalaPOW
description: Notes The KoalaPOW is a drop in the Battle for the Nexus dungeon, which was released for a limited time in September 2013 and occasionally reappears during events.

name: Kogbold Cower Shield
description: Notes This shield provides no innate DEF bonus and relatively low damage despite its armor-piercing shots. In exchange, however, it grants a significant DEF buff to you and the group, analogous to the Marble Seal. For robe classes, the +15 DEF is approximately equal to Armored, and it stacks with Armored. It also gives some SPD and VIT on equip.
effects: Damage Reduction: On ability use, you receive 70% damage for 5 seconds Shots hit multiple targets Ignores defense of target
On ability use: Gain DEF Boost (+15 DEF) to self and nearby players for 4 seconds.On ability use below 50% health: Grant DEF Boost (+20 DEF) defense instead of the usual 15.

name: Krathana
description: Notes Although its range surpasses that of the Ray Katana, the pitiful damage on this vanity item makes it impractical for actual combat.

name: Kusanagi
description: Notes A remarkably powerful end-game Katana which has unrivaled damage among all piercing Katanas except Valor, which will always comfortably outdamage it, even when affected by the Valor’s ATT reduction proc.

name: Kyuuketsuki Hitogoroshi
description: Notes Features an unconventional shot pattern in which the projectile first travels backwards and then quickly swings forward, giving it a range of roughly 6 tiles. Its damage output is not too bad either, sitting around the DPS of the tier 14 Kusanagi if you can land shots consistently.
effects: Shoots backwards Shots hit multiple targets Shots boomerang Bleeding Causes up to 80 damage for 3 seconds per stack.

name: Laborer's Hard Hat
description: Notes This is a Construction Gear item.

name: Laser Pointer
description: Notes The Laser Pointer sacrifices some range in exchange for high firerate and armor piercing shots. It also fires a secondary shot on an amplitude, which pierces enemies.

name: Leaf Bow
description: Notes This bow sacrifices its ability to pierce multiple enemies in exchange for longer range (1.75x the true range of tiered bows, plus full benefit from Inspired) and high DPS. While it is worse than endgame options like the Void Bow or Warmonger, the leaf bow is a strong option for the midgame. It also gives a hefty speed boost, allowing its user to dodge more or run faster, given the Archer’s and Huntress’ low base SPD.

name: Leaf Dragon Hide Armor
description: Notes This armor is an interesting piece of equipment that, in comparison to the T14 Wyrmhide Armor, sacrifices 6 DEF and 6 DEX for 50 HP, 6 SPD, and 5 VIT, making it a rather situational armor to use. However, its proc gives it the ability to periodically grant a large amount of DEF when using abilities, mitigating the loss somewhat.
effects: On Ability Use: DEF Boost (+15 DEF) for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 15 seconds

name: Legacy Sentient Staff
description: Notes The Legacy Sentinent Staff has the same average damage as the Staff of the Cosmic Whole but offers an additional +10 HP, +5 MP and +10 WIS. The additional stats are impressive, but the amplitude and frequency of the staff (similar to the Staff of Esben) make it a lot more challenging to hit enemies than with traditional staves. The shots form a sort of figure eight pattern with two convergence points, at roughly four and eight tiles distance, so if your targeted enemy can be safely maintained at either of these ranges the staff becomes more viable.

name: Loaded Core
description: Notes While not providing any stats by itself, this ring provides significant stat boosts when worn with other Alien Gear, reaching its full potential with an Alien Weapon and a Protection Matrix. While it gives up the triggered effects of the other four cores, its stats at full power arguably outshine every other ring in the game, and are well-balanced enough that every class can use it well. More often than not, the bonus is worth giving up one’s Weapon and Armor slot.
effects: No stat bonuses unless worn as part of set

name: Loch Sheath
description: Notes The tornadoes summoned by this weapon fire 5 shots every second, for a total of 40 shots over their lifetime. This means that each tornado can deal an average of 3000 armor piercing damage, however they only have about 5 tiles of range so you need to be in katana range for them to shoot. There is a maximum of 3 tornadoes at any given time, similar to other summons.

Only at very high DEF does this wand get beat by other ranged wands. This wand doesn’t pierce, but its spray of shots and superb rate of fire still make it a somewhat viable choice for clearing hordes of monsters.

name: Mace of the Depths
description: Notes This mace is one of two that inflict a status effect, this one being Slowed. As the Slowed duration lasts longer than the spider’s attack delay, it can be used to permanently slow enemies. In addition to its slowing shot, the spider minion possesses a secondary, rapid explosive attack. These explosions make it great for crowd-clearing, and increase overall DPS past mediocre levels.

Its straightforward shot pattern allows usage at range with minimal struggle, with all projectiles despawning at the same point.

description: Notes This is an incredibly unique orb. Rather than putting enemies into Stasis, it summons a stationary Aether Trap at the cursor location. The duration of the status effects last long enough to permanently affect anything in the trap’s radius until the trap vanishes.

Watch out for Speedy - you should be careful not to run into anything dangerous.

description: Notes Good early-game dagger. It comes from an easy dungeon so it’s really accessible, but it’s not advisable using it since there are better choices. But if you manage to find one with the special enchant on it becomes much better (as explained below).

name: Polarity Poison
description: Notes This poison throws an orange primary bomb that, on impact, creates 13 secondary blue bombs which radiate outwards in an X pattern (3 on each arm and 1 in the center), each one with the same radius as the original. The orange bomb deals exclusively (but much higher) poison damage over time, and the blue bombs deal both poison and impact damage while inflicting Slowed onto all enemies hit.

stats.damage: Primary Bomb: 5 (+0.2 per WIS over 50) % Secondary Bombs: 0 %

name: Polarity Poison (Rehearsal)
description: Notes In comparison to the original Polarity Poison the damage values are greatly reduced, but the equip stats, slow duration, and damage boost remains the same. Although it might still be a solid option for a poison, it’s rather difficult to obtain due to the limited availability of the Rehearsal Shatters. And thus this item will most likely stay a vanity item for a while.

stats.damage: Primary Bomb: 5 (+0.2 per WIS over 50) % Secondary Bombs: 0 %

name: Pollen Incendiary
description: Notes Don’t be fooled by the wisdom scaling on Pollen Incendiary’s base damage - the overwhelming majority of this poison’s damage comes from its Pollen Fallout effect. Upon use, any leftover mana is consumed in batches of 60mp, with each batch spawning additional fallout. Each instance of fallout hits enemies within the area of effect 10 times over several seconds (avg 1,600 damage each if all hit). This process occurs up to twelve times, capping the total mana cost at 840mp (or lower with MP cost reducers such as an MP Cost Reduction enchantment or The Twilight Gemstone).
effects: Arcane Nourishment: Consumes most of your available MP upon casting the poison. The greater the amount of MP consumed the more potent the explosion. Pollen Fallout: Pollen lingers within 10 squares of ground zero, dealing 120-200 damage.

name: Polygonal Garbs
description: Notes Part of the Cuboid Necromancer Set.
effects: With full set: Squarrier deals twice the damage and lasts twice as long

name: Primal Arcana
description: Notes This very unique orb doesn’t inflict Stasis, but on activation it fires a solid wall of shots that deals a good amount of damage and applies Curse to enemies hit. This is useful for easily applying Curse to enemies when Stasis is unusable or unhelpful, such as in most boss fights. This orb is able to Curse some entities that cannot be Cursed by the area-of-effect from tiered orbs, such as the breakable walls in Oryx’s Chamber or Parasite Chambers.
effects: Your weapon fires two (33% chance for third bullet) more bullets, each dealing 100-120 damage with cooldown 0.2 (0 for third bullet) seconds Curse for 5s Shots hit multiple targets Shots pass through obstacles

name: Prism of Apparitions

name: Prism of Dancing Swords
description: Notes This prism is most often used because of its stat boosts, as most other prisms don’t provide any relevant boosts. Its ability to produce two different copies of the Trickster at the time length of the Hallucination Prism is also very handy, and proves to be the most effective prism if the Trickster using it is trying to create Decoys to distract enemies, making this prism a possible primary ability depending on your playstyle. However, it is still advised to bring a T0 Decoy Prism for its ability to teleport for only 60 Mana.
effects: No teleport If used when moving, spawns two moving decoys in opposite directions If used when standing still, spawns two moving decoys in random directions

name: Prism of Dire Instability
description: Notes This unique prism does not teleport the player, but instead sends out a short-lived decoy that will explode into a dense ring of bullets at the end of its lifetime.

name: Q.U.I.V.E.R.
description: Notes A unique quiver that fires a very long-ranged Paralyze shot that starts off stationary but rapidly accelerates in the firing direction, leaving behind a trail of Slowing bullets. Alongside the Embellished Quiver and Archerang, this quiver is capable of inflicting both Paralyzed and Slowed at the same time.
effects: Inflicts Paralyzed for 4 seconds Shots hit multiple targetsAcceleration: 30 tiles/sec2 after 0.5sMaximum Speed: 18 tiles/sec Effect: Leaves behind a trail of 4 additional slowing projectiles after accelerating Trailing shots inflict Slowed for 4 seconds

name: Quartermaster Scabbard
description: Notes The projectiles begin 2 tiles behind the player, fire forward 3 tiles in front of the player, then return appoximately 8.52 tiles behind the player. This makes it very difficult to hit enemies without dashing through them. The decoy left behind can be very useful in the same situations as the Brain of the Golem prism, although it does last 3 seconds fewer. The healing effect is applied on every dash, so with proper timing the user can give themselves permanent healing provided they aren’t quieted or silenced.
effects: Shots hit multiple targets Shots boomerangOn Channeling: Summons a stationary decoyOn Dash: Healing for 5 seconds

name: Queen's Stinger
description: Notes The Queen’s Stinger is the only dagger in the game that both completely ignores the defense of the target and pierces through enemies. As a result, this dagger is useful in Tombs and The Shatters where high defense enemies are found. It is also effective at realm events such as the Cube God or Skull Shrine where it can be used to shred through large swarms of satellite minions.

name: Quiver of Shadows
description: Notes A powerful quiver that fires 6 shots, all of which ignore the target’s DEF, but with no status effect. If all 6 shots hit, this quiver is one of the best damage abilities in the game. For damage per mana, it’s on-par with the Tablet, better than the Recurring Terror Spell, and better than all other quivers. Even when you don’t hit every shot, it is a powerful ability. The downside is the short true range, lack of status effect, and no DEX boost. It also comes at a minor speed penalty.

name: Ray Katana
description: Notes A rare Katana, this weapon sacrifices some damage in exchange for slightly higher fire rate and a lot of extra range. This allows users to strike enemies from a safer distance. In addition, its faster projectile speed allows it to hit moving targets more easily. This katana is mostly useful when the enemies are very dangerous to get close to (for example, Oryx 2) and the extra damage on the Tiered ones would not be worth the risk.

name: Reaper's Shroud
description: Notes Part of the Spectral Sentry Rogue Set.

name: Recurring Terror Spell
description: Notes This is the first and only spell to have a parametric shot pattern. When fired, the shots revolve erratically around the point of casting for a few seconds, dealing damage to the first enemy that they touch. Their lingering and ultimately converging nature gives it higher accuracy, especially against stationary bosses or ones with predictable patterns (like Son of Arachna or Archdemon Malphas). By spamming the spell in the path of the boss, you can lure them into “minefields” of shots to incur large amounts of damage.
effects: Shots pass through obstacles Shots are parametric

name: Ring of Cubed Wisdom
description: Notes Note that the bonus only applied to flat wisdom bonuses from items and enchants - Relative WIS% enchants do not work. Nonetheless, this is a potent ring to convert excess WIS into strong stat bonuses. A wizard using T7 Spell, T14 Robe and this ring without any enchants or other bonuses already has +20 WIS, translating into +35 HP, +50 MP and +3 of every other stat, nearly tying with the Exalt-tier Amulet of Restoration, and can be further augmented with strong WIS and WIS tradeoff enchants.
effects: +5 WIS, +175% HP / +250% MP / +15% DEF / +15% SPD / +15% ATT / +15% DEX / +15% VIT Equal to bonus WIS

name: Ring of Decades
description: Notes This item is one of the most popular high-end rings since it provides a large amount of HP, tied with the T7 Ring of Transcendent Health. Although the Ring of Decades does not have that ring’s additional MP bonus, it is tradable and drops from an easier dungeon, albeit very rarely. It is often used as a form of wealth storage by players, due to how much these rings tend to be worth.

name: Ring of Unbound Health
description: Notes In Patch 27.7.X2 (Aug 2016), the Tier 6 Health Ring was made the Ring of Decades, while the Ring of Unbound Health was made untiered and removed from drop tables, although it was still tradable. In Patch X.18.0 (Oct 2017), the Ring of Unbound Health was returned to its former status as the T6 ring and returned to drop tables, while the Ring of Decades was made untiered and no longer dropped from enemies. In Patch X.31.9.0 (Aug 2019), the Ring of Decades was added again as a rare drop from Crystals in the Fungal Cavern.

name: Royal Guard's Cuirass
description: Notes The Cuirass not only provides fairly strong defensive stats, but it gives the incredibly powerful Invulnerable buff for 1.6 seconds when shooting while In Combat. While there is a 20 second cooldown, the power of invulnerability alone makes this a top-tier defensive armor.

name: Royal Guard's Cuirass (Rehearsal)
description: Notes In comparison to the original Royal Guard’s Cuirass, the passive proc is completely removed, but the stats remain the same. Although it might still be a solid option for armor, it’s rather difficult to obtain due to the limited availability of the Rehearsal Shatters. And thus this item will most likely stay a vanity item for a while.

Ignores defense of target Shots hit multiple targets

name: Runes of Knowledge
description: Notes Damage from the Rune Carvings ignores enemy defense. Provided an enemy remains in the radius of the runes, the single-target damage is greater than T7 Burning Retribution Spell while also being less affected by enemy DEF. Additionally, due to the initial explosion of shots and faster attack speed of the Runes compared to Sporous Spray Spell, less damage will be lost on enemies which do leave the radius.
effects: Weak for 3 seconds Shots pass through obstacles Rune Carvings: Conjure mysterious runes that deal 110-160 (135) damage every 0.6 seconds to enemies within 5 sqrs, lasting 6 seconds.

name: Rusted Barrel
description: Notes Because the poison stats are exactly the same as the Plague Poison, it can be considered an alternative drop.

name: Rusted Mechanism
description: Notes With a whopping +8 to ATT and DEX, this ring provides the highest unconditional DPS stat boost of any single ring in the game, surpassing even the Chrysalis of Eternity. However, this comes at the cost of a lack of HP along with a significant DEF penalty, making it less useful if one is unwilling to sacrifice survivability for offense.

name: Rusty Cuffs
description: Notes A rather balanced ring that functions similarly to the Experimental Ring. Not necessarily the best ring, but it is useful on most classes. MP reliant classes may suffer from the lack of any magic-related bonuses, however. This ring can serve a fantastic intermediary (or permanent) purpose on a maxed Priest- the extra def and HP boost survivability, and the speed is useful for dodging.

The time between the bursts can be decreased (up to a cap) through anything that increases fire rate, such as DEX or the Berserk buff.

name: Scepter of Devastation
description: Notes This scepter focuses on single-target damage. Although it has poor lightning damage and is limited to a few targets, its redeeming feature is the shockblast. Uniquely, the shockblast scales with Attack. On a Sorcerer maxed in both ATT and WIS, this will be the highest DPS scepter (against 1-2 targets).

stats.damage: 200 (+10 per WIS over 50) - 20 for each subsequent target

name: Scepter of Fulmination
description: Notes Although the Scepter of Fulmination does noticeably less damage (150 damage and 5 targets) than the T6 Scepter of Storms (300 damage and 5 targets), it is the only scepter that can Slow enemies, making it a great choice for any dungeon where Slow is helpful. Examples include the Abyss and the Fungal Cavern. The Scepter of Fulmination also scales about 40% better than the tiered scepters (+1 targets per 6 Wisdom over 50 vs +1 targets per 10 Wisdom over 50), which aids its total damage and the amount of enemies it can Slow.

stats.damage: 150 (+6 per WIS over 50) - 30 for each subsequent target

name: Scepter of Rust
description: Notes This Scepter is one of an elusive few items that can inflict the Weak status on enemies. Weak reduces the affected enemy’s damage output by 10%, and no enemy in the game is immune to weak. It shines in dangerous dungeons, where enemies can do hundreds of damage per hit. Because of the nature of Scepters, it can apply the debuff to multiple targets from long range, and it boasts a relatively cheap MP cost to make the debuff spammable.

stats.damage: 300 (+5 per WIS over 50) - 25 for each subsequent target

If you are the only Paladin in a group, then it’s usually better to apply Damaging and HP Boost over this seal’s buffs.

name: Shield of Flowing Clarity
description: Notes This unconventional shield is tied with the T0 Wooden Shield for having the 2nd lowest MP cost of a shield (only behind Champion’s Bastion), but unlike most other shields it Slows its targets instead of Stunning them. While this makes it less practical in situations that call specifically for Stuns, this shield has value against foes that are immune to Stuns, allowing Knights to continue inflicting status effects onto them. Since Knight is rather slow to begin with, this shield can help tackle faster enemies by slowing them down.
effects: Slowed for 4 seconds Shots hit multiple targets Shots pass through obstacles

name: Shield of Jawgmur
description: Notes Unlike any other reskin in the game, this reskin works differently than the original item, scaling off of WIS instead of ATT. As WIS is easier to build a lot of than ATT, this can work in its favor, especially with the Boundless Vessel, which is already a very solid pick for maintaining Armor Break for players with weaker pets.
effects: Armor Broken for 4 seconds Shots hit multiple targets Shots pass through obstacles

name: Shield of Ogmur
description: Notes The Shield of Ogmur is a cosmically rare UT drop that inflicts Armor Broken, a powerful buff for group DPS. It is one of 2 practical items that Armor Break, with the other being the similarly rare Crystallised Fang’s Venom. It is especially good on high DEF bosses like the Avatar of the Forgotten King. Its shield damage is also very respectable, being in between a T6 and T7 shield.

name: Soul's Guidance
description: Notes This wand is very unique compared to most other wands. On its own, it’s just a slight alternative to a typical tiered wand (with the same average DPS as the Wand of Deep Sorcery) with a wavy shot pattern. The special effect gives you a chance to spawn a soul that deals 200 flat damage every second, as an instant zap to the nearest target. Due to the wavy shot pattern and auto-aim of the souls, this is a weapon that doesn’t require as much attention from you to aim, so you can focus more on dodging.

name: Spiteful Scutum
description: Notes Compared to the Mithril Shield, its DEF is equal as well as its average damage. In addition, this shield gives better range, armor piercing, an extra second of stun and costing 5 less MP, as well as an extra 4 ATT/SPD. As tiered shields increase in MP but not stun duration, and no other UT has a longer stun, this UT is the most efficient MP/Stun Shield available. In addition to the stun, the shots deal the 2nd highest damage of any shield - only beaten by Oryx’s Escutcheon’s exalted beams.

However, taking damage to under 80% maximum HP activates Mad God’s Rage, increasing DPS stats by 10. This tends to work best when you’d take lots of damage, anyways (like the Lost Halls or Oryx’s Sanctuary).

name: Staff of the Crystal Serpent
description: Notes Its damage per shot is equal to that of the T7 Staff of Destruction, but its increased fire rate makes its DPS in between the T8 Staff of Horror and the T9 Staff of Necrotic Arcana, against an enemy with either 0 or more than 60 defense. It also has one of the best range of all staves, tied with the Tezcacoatl’s Tail, being able to hit enemies at the same range as wands. It has its uses for Wine Cellars because you can easily land a lot of shots on the boss with extra safety.

name: Staff of the Eclipse
description: History Before Exalt Version 5.15.0.0 (Aug 2025), this item dealt 65-110 damage.

name: Staff of the Fundamental Core
description: Notes The strongest tiered staff, making it the definitive end-game equip on any staff class. It is especially an appreciable upgrade over the T13 Staff of the Vital Unity, sporting +15 minimum damage and +5 maximum damage for +10 average damage, the biggest jump in power since the increase from T6 to T7 staff (+12.5 average damage). While a few UT staves can outdamage it in certain situations, this staff combines high damage, high range, and ease of use.

name: Staff of the Rising Sun
description: Notes This is a reskinned version of T12 Staff of the Cosmic Whole, released during the June 2014 Month of the Mad God event. During the event, the item dropped in the same locations as its tiered equivalent.

name: Staff of the Saint
description: History Released in Patch X.32.7.1 (Mar 2020).

name: Staff of the Vital Unity
description: Notes The T13 staff is a consistent damage dealer with no gimmicks or caveats. While being around 10% weaker than the T14 staff and around 30% weaker than Superior in terms of DPS, it is significantly easier to obtain and still deals decent damage.

name: Staff of Unholy Sacrifice
description: Notes Upon first glance, this staff appears to be blatantly overpowered. It shoots 4 projectiles in a cone dealing enormous damage, while also piercing through targets at a large range.

name: Staff of Yuletide Carols
description: Notes The Staff of Yuletide Carols has stats equivalent to the T11 Staff of Astral Knowledge, and does fire two projectiles despite some initial mistaken belief that it had only one projectile and might therefore do less damage than its tier 11 counterpart.

name: Star of Enlightenment
description: Notes An interesting star that grants Berserk instead of the usual Speedy, massively boosting the ninjas DPS on top of a massive DEX boost. While an obvious detriment, the passive speed reduction can work in your favour in other areas where micrododging is more important than running away, such as that of the Moonlight Village. While your ability key is held down, your pet is still able to regenerate your MP, and as such is easier to sustain its effects.

name: Stasis Orb
description: Notes This is a starter ability for the Mystic class and does not drop from enemies.
effects: On enemies: Stasis and Curse Stasis Radius: 3 tiles Stasis Duration: 3 seconds Curse Radius: 5 (+0.1 per WIS over 50) tiles Curse Duration: 2 (+1 per 10 WIS over 50) seconds

name: Steadfast Glyph
description: Notes Part of the Earthen Bulwark Summoner Set.
effects: Runic Stone: Increases Stonemould Mace summon damage to 800-1200, health to 2000, and defense to 30.

name: Steam Pipe
description: Notes The Steam Pipe fires four decelerating shots at once in a line; two piercing water shots, and two armor piercing fire shots. Each subsequent shot has a faster deceleration, and thus a shorter range. Shots will reach their final destination at roughly the same time. Like many decelerating weapons, the Steam Pipe is great against enemies who chase you.
effects: Shots hit multiple targets Acceleration: -22.2/-50 tiles/sec2
Ignores defense of target Acceleration: -33.3/-100 tiles/sec2

name: Storied Blades
description: Notes At a glance, it appears as a sidegrade to the Star of Enlightenment, giving the user damaging and an ATT boost instead. But on closer inspection, meeting the above proc thresholds allows the user to deal additional damage in the form of extra projectiles. What the items tooltip fails to mention however is that any MP regeneration (including your own pet) is disabled while the ability is held down. Thus, this item is best used when you expect to get hit frequently.

name: Storm Caller
description: Notes Because the scepter stats are exactly the same as the Scepter of Fulmination, it can be considered an alternative drop.

name: Superior
description: Notes Unlike other staves with a higher shot count than a tiered variant, Superior has no decrease in fire-rate. The primary drawback comes from its limited range (6.22 tiles, with a true range of about 5 tiles) with swerving shots that make it hard to aim.

name: Sword of Majesty
description: Notes The most powerful tiered sword, it beats all but the Sword of the Colossus and a point-blank Pixie-Enchanted Sword, Demon Blade and Cheerful Chipper. Unlike the latter swords, the Sword of Majesty compromises nothing for its staggering damage output.

name: Sword of the Colossus
description: Notes This powerful sword outdamages all tiered swords, all tiered flails, and the majority of untiered swords. Divinity deals more single-target DPS, but only if you aren’t berserked. In addition, it has a range of 4.5 tiles, much higher than its alternatives.

name: Tablet of the King's Avatar
description: Notes The Tablet is a highly valuable spell which deals massive damage. The Tablet’s shots travel a single tile before converging back to the point of origin. This makes it easier to land the ‘perfect spellbomb’ on most enemies.

name: Taiko Drums
description: Notes A lute with 2 modes. Like the Kogbold Multitool, you can swap abilities by holding Shift. Both modes give Inspired to allies, but give a special version of Inspired to the user which modifies your shots differently than normal.
effects: Within 4.5 tiles Inspired for 6.5 seconds. Tempo Change: Control the rhythm of the drums to match the situation Ability 1: Crescendo Effect on self: Berserk for 5 seconds. Blitz Rhythm: Greatly increases shot speed, whilst boosting effective weapon range Ability 2: Adagio Party Effect: Healing [40/s] (+1 per WIS over 75) for 4 seconds within 5.5 sqrs Tranquil Rhythm: Lowers your shot speed, but boosts effective weapon range

name: Tezcacoatl's Tail
description: Notes This staff possesses the third highest damage per shot of any staff and the unique property of ignoring its target’s defense, at the cost of a massively reduced rate of fire. As such, it is most useful in situations where you would need to pack a lot of damage into single shots, or against enemies with very high DEF or the tendency to Armor themselves.

name: Tezutsu Hanabi
description: Notes The Friendly Spirit summoned by this ability will level up in proximity with those summoned by the Sage’s Wakibiki, Ethereal Happi, Flowering Kimono, and Kagenohikari. The level of the spirit will increase further as more spirits are gathered.
effects: Hold [Shift] for 0.75 seconds to change modes Light Show: On Ability Use: Summon an orbiting Friendly Spirit which chases and attacks enemies. The spirit synergizes in the presence of other spirits. Upgrade Levels: 1: 150-200 damage shotgun. 2: 150-200 damage shotgun and infrequent 70-100 damage novas. 3: 150-200 damage shotgun and infrequent 70-100 damage novas. Lifetime: 6 / 8 / 10 seconds Cooldown: 10 seconds Fireworks Stockpile: On Shoot: Build up a stockpile which invigorates and strengthens nearby spirits. Cooldown: 5 seconds

name: The Twilight Gemstone
description: Notes The Twilight Gemstone offers a very noticeable MP cost reduction of 20%. This allows it to be an amazing ring for both utility and DPS. For long distance rushes, like realm clearing, the Trickster appreciates the ability to teleport more often (and a +5 SPD boost). For group healing, there’s no better ring for the Priest.

name: Tipsy Topper

The main drawback is that this helm also inflicts Unstable on the user, making precise aiming and landing shots much harder on most melees without being dangerously close to an enemy.

name: Tlatoani's Shroud
description: Notes An unconventional robe which serves as a spiritual successor to the Robe of the Tlatoani, granting a whopping +16 WIS and the highest MP bonus of any robe (+180). This makes it incredible if one is solely interested in repeatedly casting wis-boosted abilities, but the lack of any defense hurts this robe quite a bit.

name: Token of Happiness
description: Notes This ring is the only realistic item in the game to apply Energized. It can be used as a swapout, to increase MP regen when bosses are invulnerable. Be careful of the Slowed you get, especially as the statuses from the proc last 0.7 seconds even after you stop shooting.
effects: Happy Spirit: When shooting, gain Energized and Slowed.

name: Token of Warmth
description: Notes While this ring may seem weak at first glance due to the slow on shoot, it is incredibly powerful within the Moonlight Village or other encounters with high bullet density, due to lower speed being a benefit in most phases.

name: Tome of Holy Furor
description: Notes This tome sacrifices all personal healing and team HP regen buffs in exchange for a hefty AoE DEX Boost (+12 DEX). This greatly increases group DPS and stacks with Berserk to push damage to ridiculous amounts, while granting the user extra shots while DEX boosted for even more damage. As a tradeoff for not healing, it provides +60 HP and +6 DEX when equipped. Needless to say, it should be used when damage is a priority over healing, such as when trying to push a boss into the next phase or if there are already other priests focused on healing. The lack of a self-heal makes this tome more

A maxed Priest will often have defenses in the 60+ range, which is comparable to a heavy armor class. (Note that Armored does not increase the In Combat threshold.)

name: Vest of Abandoned Shadows
description: Notes This armor bears similarity to the Leaf Dragon Hide Armor, although this armor provides slightly higher DEF, a massive bump to VIT, but no SPD bonus. The proc is also slightly different from Leaf Hide’s proc, providing Armored instead of +15 DEF, and activating when In Combat instead of activating upon ability use.

Although it might still be a solid armor, it’s rather difficult to obtain due to the limited availability of the Rehearsal Shatters. And thus this item will most likely stay a vanity item for a while.

name: Vesture of Duality
description: Notes With a total of +20 ATT with its proc active, this robe is the best robe for DPS in the game. It also gives a nice SPD boost, which helps naturally slow classes like the Wizard. In exchange, this robe sacrifices DEF, MP, and WIS. In endgame content (where this robe is most likely to be used), a few extra DEF is unlikely to save you. The massive attack boost usually makes up for the other stats.

It deals, on average, as much damage as the T5 Magic Nova Spell, offering a large VIT increase but with a slightly increased MP cost. Also of note is the decreased shot speed; the projectiles travel at only 6 tiles/second instead of the 16 tiles/second of other spells.

name: Volcanic Sheath
description: Notes Volcanic Sheath is the only sheath which possesses wismod, boosting its own damage output as the user’s wisdom increases, as every 5 wisdom boosts its damage and radius. With maxed wisdom and no other items boosting it, kensei can reach 71 wisdom, making the explosion do 600 instead of 400 damage. This may be useful when clearing larger groups of enemies, as dashing through them and landing behind them will provide the user with stacks to increase damage output when the dash ends.

While cursed is harder to self inflict on Sorcerer and Summoner unless you use Bloodroot Extract. It can easily be acheived and is encouraged on Priest where Necronomicon which also drops from the same location gives cursed on self on ability use.

name: Wand of the Bulwark
description: Notes The projectiles this weapon fires are kind of like the little floating projectiles fired by sandsman sorcerers, clockwork golems, and Red Demons. They float around lazily in a figure-eight pattern and last a very long time. You can’t shoot them at something; you basically need to create a minefield with them and attract your prey into the field.
effects: Shots are parametric Shots pass through obstacles Shots hit multiple targets

name: Wand of the Fallen
description: Notes The Wand of the Fallen is a strong midgame wand that sacrifices range and enemy piercing for firepower. It will completely outdamage the Conducting Wand and T14 Wand of Retribution at all DEF levels, making it great if you can afford to get close. Unlike many other DPS wands, this wand fires straight and with a sane projectile velocity.

name: Wand of the Summoned One
description: Notes This is an Antinomy Gear item. It is a reskin of Wand of Recompense with an extra 2 attack on equip.

name: Wandering Souls Spell
description: Notes Although this spell only fires 6 shots (compared to 20 for tiered spells), it more than makes up for this with its other attributes. Most obviously, the damage for each individual shot is significantly higher, so much so that it does slightly more total damage on average than even the T6 Elemental Detonation Spell for only marginally more MP, without even considering that concentrating the damage into fewer projectiles means that enemy defense is not as effective. On top of this, the ability of the projectiles to pierce and ignore terrain, and especially their relatively large size and u

name: Warmonger
description: Notes At first glance, this bow is incredible. It outdamages every bow at low to mid DEF, being only beaten by a 5-shot Predator Bow and the undisputed best damaging bow Makakoyumi. It has one of the highest ranges of bows at 8 tiles. Unlike tiered bows, the arc gap is small enough to consistently make use of the full normal range. While the Warmonger’s true range isn’t boosted by the Bard’s Inspired, the range still surpasses its endgame competitor, the Bow of the Void. It is also great for crowd control, piercing enemies and passing through obstacles.

name: Warped Mantle
description: Notes This armor has defense in between T8 and T9 armor, though it provides Healing. This makes it a better armor than most tiered armors for Knights, which value the healing more than a few points of defense (which they have enough of). This armor is relatively useless for Paladins or when playing with Paladins & Priests, as their abilities provide Healing already.
effects: On Shoot: When in combat, grants Healing for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds

name: Water Dragon Silk Robe
description: Notes This is a highly versatile robe that compared to the Robe of the Star Mother sacrifices 65 MP, 2 DEF and 7 WIS for a nice increase of 6 SPD, 1 ATT, and 5 VIT. It also has a powerful reactive proc that can restore large amounts of HP when using an ability below 80% HP.
effects: On Ability Use when below 80% HP: Heal 90 HP and gain Healing [30/s] for 3 seconds.Cooldown: 15 seconds

name: Wavecrest Concertina
description: Notes A DPS-oriented item for an otherwise support-oriented class, the parrots that are spawned by either using the ability or through its proc will inflict large amounts of DEF-piercing damage to enemies. The proc is particularly effective if paired with a high rate-of-fire weapon such as the Thousand Shot, DEX-boosting equipment/consumables (ex. Effusions of Dexterity), or buffs such as Berserk (from Warriors). The tradeoff of the massive DPS capabilities of this item is that it does not inflict DEF Boost on the user, unlike tiered lutes.
effects: Party Effect: Within 4.5 squares Inspired for 4 seconds. Parrot Cannon: Throws a fighting parrot at your target. Parrot Throw Time: 0.6s Fighting Parrot: 450 damage within 2 squares every 1 second. (1800 maximum total) Fighting Parrot Effect: Ignores defense of target

name: Wretched Rags
description: Notes This unique robe focuses primarily on survivability, increasing the wearer’s maximum HP equal to their max MP stat, at the cost of halving their base HP and making all +HP bonuses effectively half as strong. Without any other equipment, this robe is a net +25 HP on Bard and +50 HP on other robe classes, but investment into max MP via a combination of MP-boosting equipment and enchantments can provide the largest HP pools to robe classes.
